import re

def trim_edges(s):
    # remove leading/trailing whitespace
    s = s.strip()
    # remove edge quotes and punctuation from both ends
    # define characters to trim
    trim_left = set('««""“”\'' + ' (')  # maybe not needed
    trim_right = set('»،:.;!?…»"»)]»»»»"')
    # We'll do more robust: while first or last char in a set
    while s and s[0] in '««"\'“”«»()[]{}<>':
        s = s[1:]
    while s and s[-1] in '«»"\'.,:;!?…)]»»»':
        s = s[:-1]
    return s

ayah1 = "إِنَّ عِبَادِي لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَانٌ إِلَّا مَنِ اتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ الْغَاوِينَ"
ayah2 = "إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ لَهُ سُلْطَانٌ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَلَى رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ"
start_q = text.find('"إن الشيطان لا يسلِّط على أحدكم فيفتنه في دينه')
hadith_text = text[start_q+1:]  # until end
hadith_text_trim = trim_edges(hadith_text)
(start_q, len(text), hadith_text_trim[:100])

start_quote = 398
span_start_hadith = start_quote + 1
span_end_hadith = len(text)
span_text_hadith = hadith_text_trim
span_start_hadith, span_end_hadith, repr(span_text_hadith[:120])

# Compute hadith span with exact matching trimming
start_q = text.find('"إن الشيطان لا يسلِّط على أحدكم فيفتنه في دينه')
hadith_start = start_q + 1  # after opening quote
hadith_text_raw = text[hadith_start:]
# We'll trim whitespace only at the ends for display purposes
def trim_edges_strict(s):
    s = s.strip()
    # also drop leading/trailing punctuation characters from the ends
    while s and s[0] in '««"\'“”':
        s = s[1:]
    while s and s[-1] in '،؛:.,!؟…»»»"»':
        s = s[:-1]
    return s

hadith_text_display = trim_edges_strict(hadith_text_raw)

# Now locate a substring in raw that equals hadith_text_display
pos = text.find(hadith_text_display, hadith_start)
pos, hadith_start

end_match = 399 + len(hadith_text_display)
span_start_hadith = 399
span_end_hadith = end_match
span_text_hadith = hadith_text_display
